name: Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry
region: Scotland (exact location Unplottable and hidden by enchantment)
description: A castle with towers, dungeons, and moving staircases, hidden from Muggles and reachable by the Hogwarts Express from Platform Nine and Three-Quarters.
notableFeatures: enchanted ceiling in the Great Hall
protective wards concealing it from Muggles

subLocations.name: The Great Hall
subLocations.description: The main dining hall, with four house tables and a ceiling bewitched to show the sky outside.

subLocations.name: Gryffindor Tower
subLocations.description: Gryffindor's common room and dormitories, entered through the Fat Lady's portrait.

subLocations.name: Slytherin dungeon common room
subLocations.description: Slytherin's common room, below the lake, entered via a concealed dungeon wall.

subLocations.name: The Room of Requirement
subLocations.description: A room that appears only when someone walks past it three times needing it badly, taking the form of that need.

subLocations.name: The Forbidden Forest
subLocations.description: A dense forest on the school grounds, off-limits to students, home to centaurs, Acromantulas, and other creatures.

subLocations.name: The Chamber of Secrets
subLocations.description: A hidden chamber built by Salazar Slytherin, entered via the girls' second-floor bathroom, home to a Basilisk.

subLocations.name: The Astronomy Tower
subLocations.description: The school's tallest tower, used for Astronomy classes.

subLocations.name: The Hospital Wing
subLocations.description: The school infirmary, run by Madam Pomfrey.

subLocations.name: The Library
subLocations.description: The school library, including a Restricted Section requiring permission to access.

subLocations.name: The Headmaster's Office
subLocations.description: Reached via a spiral staircase behind a stone gargoyle that guards the entrance with a password.

subLocations.name: The Black Lake
subLocations.description: A great lake on the grounds, home to the Giant Squid and merpeople.

name: Diagon Alley
description: The main wizarding shopping street in London, entered through the Leaky Cauldron and opened by tapping a brick wall.

subLocations.name: Gringotts Wizarding Bank
subLocations.description: The wizarding bank, run by goblins, with vaults deep underground.

subLocations.name: Ollivanders
subLocations.description: A wand shop ('Makers of Fine Wands since 382 B.C.') run by Garrick Ollivander.

subLocations.name: Flourish and Blotts
subLocations.description: A wizarding bookshop.

subLocations.name: Madam Malkin's Robes for All Occasions
subLocations.description: A robe shop where first-years are fitted for their school uniform.

subLocations.name: Knockturn Alley
subLocations.description: A dark side-street off Diagon Alley associated with the Dark Arts.

subLocations.name: Weasleys' Wizard Wheezes
subLocations.description: A joke shop opened by Fred and George Weasley.

name: Hogsmeade
description: The only all-wizarding village in Britain; third-years and up may visit on weekends with a signed permission form.

subLocations.name: The Three Broomsticks
subLocations.description: A pub run by Madam Rosmerta.

subLocations.name: Honeydukes
subLocations.description: A sweet shop, reachable in secret via a passage from the one-eyed witch statue inside Hogwarts.

subLocations.name: The Shrieking Shack
subLocations.description: A reputedly haunted house, actually used by Remus Lupin during his transformations while at school.

subLocations.name: The Hog's Head
subLocations.description: A shabby pub run by Aberforth Dumbledore.

name: Ministry of Magic
description: The seat of wizarding government in Britain, entered by Floo, visitor's entrance telephone box, or Apparition.

subLocations.name: Department of Mysteries
subLocations.description: A secretive department researching subjects like time, death, and prophecy.

subLocations.name: Courtroom Ten
subLocations.description: The Wizengamot's courtroom used for major trials.
subLocations.source: Goblet of Fire (Pensieve memory); Order of the Phoenix

name: Godric's Hollow
description: A village where the Potters lived and died, and where Dumbledore and Bathilda Bagshot also once lived.
notableFeatures: war memorial that shifts to show the Potters

name: The Burrow
description: The ramshackle, magically-expanded home of the Weasley family.
notableFeatures: multiple stacked stories held up by magic

name: Azkaban
region: an island in the North Sea
description: The wizarding prison, formerly guarded by Dementors.
